zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Lambert於大約1210年出生在英國越可爾克雷文時，他的父親Sir Edmund Lambert31歲，母親Alice Conyers31歲。他於大約1235年與Elizabeth Clapham在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1289年享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Lambert于大约1210年出生在英国越可爾克雷文时，他的父亲Sir Edmund Lambert31岁，母亲Alice Conyers31岁。他于大约1235年与Elizabeth Clapham在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1289年享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In England, the native Old English form Landbeorht was replaced by Lambert, the Continental form of the name that was taken to England by the Normans from France. The name gained wider currency in Britain in the Middle Ages with the immigration of weavers from Flanders, among whom Saint Lambert or Lamprecht, bishop of Maastricht in around 700, was a popular cult figure. In Italy the name was popularized in the Middle Ages as a result of the fame of Lambert I and II, Dukes of Spoleto and Holy Roman Emperors. Lambert is the second most frequent surname in Wallonia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht (see also Lampert ).

History: Aubin Lambert dit Champagne from Tourouvre in Orne, France, married Élisabeth Aubert in Quebec City, QC, in 1670; see also Champagne . Eustache Lambert from Boulogne-sur-Mer in Pas-de-Calais, France, married Marie Laurence in France c. 1656, died in Quebec City, QC, in 1673. Pierre Lambert from Fourmetot in Eure, France, married Marie Normand in Quebec City, QC, in 1680. Augustin Hébert dit Lambert, a descendant of Augustin Hébert from Paris, France, married Françoise Petit in Varennes, QC, in 1761. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Lambers
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberth
meanings: English: variant of   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lambertus
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic name, Latinized form of the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lamberty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ana, Dimas, Jose, Juan, Altagracia, Angel, Blanca, Efrain, Fernando, Jaime, Ramon.
German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Lambert , from Lamberti, the genitive of its Latinized form Lambertus . Compare Lamberti 2.

variants.name: Lambertz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Johann, Ralf.
North German: patronymic from   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberto
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lampert
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Slovak; Hungarian (Lampért): variant of Lambert . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed some cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht.
English: variant of Lambard, an occupational name from Middle English lambherd, lambhird, lambhurd ‘lamb-herd’ (Old English lamb + hierde).

variants.name: Lambertsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Dutch, Danish, North German, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  . This surname has died out in the Netherlands.
History: Cornelius Lambertsen came from Utrecht, the Netherlands, arriving at New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1642.

variants.name: Lamb
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Lambert was born about 1210, in Craven, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Edmund Lambert, was 31 and his mother, Alice Conyers, was 31. He married Elizabeth Clapham about 1235, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1289, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Lambert est né environ 1210, à Craven, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edmund Lambert, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alice Conyers, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Clapham environ 1235, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé environ 1289, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Lambert um 1210 in Craven, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edmund Lambert 31 und seine Mutter Alice Conyers 31. Er hat um 1235 in Yorkshire, England Elizabeth Clapham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb um 1289 im Alter von 81 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Lambert è nato il circa 1210 nel Craven, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Edmund Lambert, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Alice Conyers, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Clapham circa 1235, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì circa 1289, all'età di 81 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Lambert(이)가 대략 1210년 영국 요르크실르 크레이븐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edmund Lambert(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Conyers(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1235년 영국 요르크실르에서 Elizabeth Clapham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1289년 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Clapham около 1235 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер около 1289 г. в возрасте 81 год.
